assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign exchange risk related to an intragroup dividend receivable.
The issue is limited to identifying IFRS hedge accounting models that are relevant from the provided context.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as one of the three hedge accounting models for recognised assets or liabilities and firm commitments.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model relevant in consolidated financial statements.
